item.title: England and Ghana play out goalless draw
item.description: England were unable to repeat their impressive attacking display in their second FIFA World Cup Group L match as they were held to a goalless draw by Ghana at Boston Stadium. The Three Lions knew a win would secure their place in the Round of 32, but they were frustrated by Ghana's strong defence and organisation.
item.content:encoded:
England were unable to repeat their impressive attacking display in their second FIFA World Cup Group L match as they were held to a goalless draw by Ghana at Boston Stadium.

The Three Lions knew a win would secure their place in the Round of 32, but they were frustrated by Ghana's strong defence and organisation.

The draw keeps England top of Group L, but they will need to wait until their final match to confirm their place in the Round of 32.

England will face Panama, while Ghana take on Croatia in their final Group L matches on Sunday at 9am.

Meanwhile, Cristiano Ronaldo became the first player to score in six FIFA World Cups after netting a double in Portugal's 5-0 win over Uzbekistan.

Panama will face Croatia in the next hour, while Colombia will take on DR Congo at 2pm.

Stay with us for updates from the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Ronaldo becomes first player to score in six World Cups as Portugal thrash Uzbekistan 5-0
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo has become the first player to score in six FIFA World Cups after netting a double in Portugal's 5-0 win over Uzbekistan. The 41-year-old opened the scoring in the sixth minute, becoming the second-oldest player to score at a World Cup.
item.content:encoded:
Cristiano Ronaldo has become the first player to score in six FIFA World Cups after netting a double in Portugal's 5-0 win over Uzbekistan.

The 41-year-old opened the scoring in the sixth minute, becoming the second-oldest player to score at a World Cup.

Nuno Mendes doubled Portugal's lead in the 17th minute before Ronaldo added his second in the 39th minute after being set up by Bruno Fernandes, giving Portugal a 3-0 lead at halftime.

In the second half, an own goal by Uzbekistan goalkeeper Abduvohid Nematov added to Portugal's advantage before substitute Rafael Leão scored a late fifth goal to complete the convincing victory.

The win moves Portugal to the top of Group K, although Colombia can overtake them if they defeat DR Congo later today.

Portugal are ranked 5th in the latest FIFA World Rankings and are making their ninth FIFA World Cup appearance. Their best result came in 1966 when they finished third.

Uzbekistan, ranked 50th in the world, are making their historic first FIFA World Cup appearance.

Portugal's final Group K match will be against Colombia on Sunday at 11.30am, while Uzbekistan will face DR Congo at the same time.

Meanwhile, in other matches, England will take on Ghana in the next hour, Panama will face Croatia at 11am, while Colombia will meet DR Congo at 2pm.

Stay with us for updates from the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Legge calls for better discipline ahead of Reds clash
Jersey donations boost Northland Rugby Union Campaign
item.description: NRU says the contribution strengthens the Union’s grassroots rugby programme and reflects the commitment of the Vanua o Nawaibuta, its Board, and its people to the development of local rugby talent. NRU acknowledged the layered investment made by Nawaibuta in supporting both the team and the broader growth of sports within the region.
item.content:encoded:
NRU says the contribution strengthens the Union’s grassroots rugby programme and reflects the commitment of the Vanua o Nawaibuta, its Board, and its people to the development of local rugby talent.

NRU acknowledged the layered investment made by Nawaibuta in supporting both the team and the broader growth of sports within the region.

They say the donation also symbolizes confidence in the ability of local talent to excel in sport, business, and community development.

Receiving the jerseys on behalf of the team, Captain Ioane Gasai expressed gratitude to Komai Nawaibuta Suliasi Daunitutu, Board Chair Tuidama, the Board, and the Vanua for hosting the team over the past month.

Gasai acknowledged God's guidance and the unity shown by the wider Northland family from Verata, Vugalei, Tai Vugalei, Namalata, Nailega, Sawakasa, Naloto, Wailevu, Nayavu, Namena, and Dawasamu.

He says support has continued to flow from communities stretching from the highlands to the shores of Vualiku kei Tailevu, including those residing and working both locally and overseas.

The Captain says that despite facing numerous social and logistical challenges, the Northland side has overcome strong opposition from the Ra, Nasinu, and Tavua provincial teams to secure a place in the Viti Levu elimination final against Rewa.

Gasai says he has invited supporters and rugby fans to attend the highly anticipated clash against Rewa at 3.00pm on Saturday, 27 June 2026, at the Dawasamu Secondary School Grounds.

The team conveyed its sincere appreciation to the elders of the Vanua and its people for their unwavering belief, support, and encouragement throughout the campaign.

item.title: Isikeli Rabitu replaces injured Habosi in Flying Fijians squad
item.description: Isikeli Rabitu has been called up to the FIJI Water Flying Fijians squad for the Nations Championship, replacing Racing 92 winger Vinaya Habosi, who has been ruled out through injury. The Swire Shipping Fijian Drua centre will join the national camp ahead of Fiji’s opening match against Wales.
item.content:encoded:
Isikeli Rabitu has been called up to the FIJI Water Flying Fijians squad for the Nations Championship, replacing Racing 92 winger Vinaya Habosi, who has been ruled out through injury.

The Swire Shipping Fijian Drua centre will join the national camp ahead of Fiji’s opening match against Wales.

The former Suva Grammar School student, Rabitu, enjoyed an outstanding season with the Drua and will be looking to carry that form into his international duties.

With just 11 days remaining before the tournament begins, Rabitu’s inclusion adds further excitement, creativity, and depth to the Flying Fijians lineup.

Fiji will take on Wales on July 4 in Cardiff, Wales, with kickoff scheduled for 1:10am.

item.title: Crusaders captain David Havili heading to Japan
Haaland scores twice as Norway beat Senegal to reach World Cup Round of 32
item.description: Erling Haaland scored a double as Norway has qualified for the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 with a thrilling 3-2 victory over Senegal in their Group I clash. Marcus Pedersen gave Norway the lead in the 43rd minute before Haaland struck twice in the second half, scoring in the 48th and 58th minutes to put his side firmly in control.
item.content:encoded:
Erling Haaland scored a double as Norway has qualified for the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 with a thrilling 3-2 victory over Senegal in their Group I clash.

Marcus Pedersen gave Norway the lead in the 43rd minute before Haaland struck twice in the second half, scoring in the 48th and 58th minutes to put his side firmly in control.

Haaland has scored four of Norway's seven goals at the FIFA World Cup 2026 so far.

Senegal responded through Ismaila Sarr, who found the net in the 53rd minute and again in stoppage time, but the comeback fell short as Norway held on for all three points.

Norway are ranked 39th in the latest FIFA World Rankings and are making their fourth FIFA World Cup appearance. Their best finish came in 1998 when they reached the Round of 16.

Senegal, ranked 19th in the world, are appearing in their fourth FIFA World Cup. Their best result came in 2022 when they also advanced to the Round of 16.

The victory sees Norway qualify for the Round of 32 alongside France, who earlier defeated Iraq 3-0 to secure their place in the knockout stage.

Norway will conclude their Group I campaign against France next Saturday at 7am, while Senegal will take on Iraq at the same time.

In other matches, Argentina defeated Austria 2-nil to also qualify for the Round of 32 while Algeria will face Jordan at 3pm.

item.title: France through to Round of 32 after 3-0 win over Iraq
item.description: France have booked their place in the Round of 32 of the FIFA World Cup 2026 after defeating Iraq 3-0 in their Group I clash. Captain Kylian Mbappé starred for the French side, scoring in the 14th and 54th minutes, while Ousmane Dembélé added the third goal in the 66th minute.
item.content:encoded:
France have booked their place in the Round of 32 of the FIFA World Cup 2026 after defeating Iraq 3-0 in their Group I clash.

Captain Kylian Mbappé starred for the French side, scoring in the 14th and 54th minutes, while Ousmane Dembélé added the third goal in the 66th minute.

France held a 1-0 lead at halftime before dominating the second half to secure a comfortable victory.

The French are ranked second in the latest FIFA World Rankings and are making their 17th FIFA World Cup appearance. They are two-time world champions, having won the title in 1998 and 2018.

The Iraqis are ranked 58th in the FIFA World Rankings and are making their second FIFA World Cup appea rance. They remain without a win after suffering their second consecutive defeat of the tournament.

France will conclude their Group I campaign against Norway at 7am on Saturday, while Iraq will face Senegal at the same time.

Meanwhile, in other matches, Argentina defeated Austria 2-0 earlier this morning.

Norway is currently leading Senegal 1-nil at halftime and Algeria will face Jordan at 3pm.

Stay with us for updates on the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Bail hearing for former Fiji football player scheduled for Monday
item.description: A former Fiji and district-level football player will have his bail hearing on Monday.The matter was called before Magistrate Yoge

item.description: Swire Shipping Fijian Drua players Virimi Vakatawa and Tuidraki Samusamuvodre have once again been named in the 24-member Barbaria

item.title: Drua Women eye home semi-final with Reds clash
item.description: The McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women’s side will be aiming for a win this Saturday when they take on the Reds, as victory would secure t

item.title: Mbappé gives France 1-nil lead before weather halts Iraq clash
item.description: France hold a 1-0 lead over Iraq in their FIFA World Cup 2026 Group I clash, although the match remains suspended due to severe we

item.title: Messi scores twice as Argentina beat Austria 2-0
item.description: Lionel Messi scored a brace as defending champions Argentina defeated Austria 2-0 in their FIFA World Cup 2026 Group J clash.Messi
